summary: Frontend-only POS smoke testing via Playwright. Login (valid+invalid), Open Shift, header info, scan flow with 4 barcodes, unknown barcode toast, stacking, F1 qty focus, F4 product search, F5 payment (cash with change + finalize + reset + new trx#), Save Pending + F7 resume, Alt+P petty cash, F12 sync, F2 logout all WORK. CRITICAL bug: Alt+R Return modal crashes the entire React tree (white screen) because completed transactions persisted via posStore.finalize do NOT contain an `items` array — items are stored in a separate Dexie table `transaction_items`, but ReturnModal reads `t.items.length` / `t.items.map(...)`. After the crash all subsequent UI is unreachable (Alt+C, status bar, F2 navigation, F12 toast).

ui_bugs.issue: Crashes with 'Cannot read properties of undefined (reading length)' the moment the modal opens — selected.items (and t.items in the transaction list) are undefined. Causes a full white-screen React error and kills the POS until reload.
Could not be tested in this run because the preceding ReturnModal crash blanks the app. Needs retest after Return fix.
Same blockage — could not be verified because Return crash unmounts the tree. Independently they need a clean retest.
Radix a11y warning repeated: 'Missing Description or aria-describedby for DialogContent'. Add <DialogDescription> or aria-describedby to each modal.

integration_issues.issue: posStore.finalize stores transaction header in db.transactions and line items in db.transaction_items separately. mockApi.listTransactions returns headers only. ReturnModal assumes header has .items — mismatch.

action_items: FIX (HIGH PRIORITY): Make ReturnModal robust. Either (a) update mockApi.listTransactions to hydrate `items` per transaction from db.transaction_items (preferred), or (b) store the items array inline in db.transactions.add({...trx, items}) in posStore.finalize, or (c) defensively guard `(t.items || []).length` / `(selected?.items || []).map(...)` in ReturnModal. Best fix: hydrate items in listTransactions so retur data is real.

RETEST after fix: Alt+R full flow (pick original trx → tick qty → reason → Proses Retur → success toast), Alt+C Close Shift (Expected = opening + cash sales + pettyIn - pettyOut, enter actual, confirm → redirect to /login), Status bar contents (ONLINE/shift/session/user/trx#), F2 logout navigation, F12 sync toast.
Add DialogDescription (or aria-describedby) to every shadcn DialogContent to silence the Radix a11y warnings.
Consider replacing `let trxCounter = 1` module-level counter in posStore.js with a per-shift persistent counter — currently TRX numbering resets on full reload.
critical_code_review_comments: posStore.finalize persists transaction.items into a sibling table (db.transaction_items) but ReturnModal/TransactionListModal expect them inline — data shape contract is inconsistent across modules. Choose one and document it.
ReturnModal.jsx has no defensive null checks on `selected.items` / `t.items` / `it.qty`. Any consumer that mis-shapes a transaction breaks the whole app.
POSPage.jsx is ~250 lines and owns 9 modal-open booleans + a 100-line keydown handler — consider extracting a useShortcuts hook and a useModalState reducer.
calcLine in posStore allows negative qty (RETURN mode) but `Math.max(0, gross - discAmt)` clamps line total to >= 0 regardless of sign — RETURN lineTotal will not be negative as expected.
trxCounter is in-memory only — page reload resets it to 1, risking duplicate trx numbers within a shift.

success_rate.frontend: ~80% (12/15 scenarios verified; 3 blocked by ReturnModal crash)

seed_data_creation: None — Dexie seedIfEmpty auto-seeds 25 products, 4 customers, 2 users on first load. IndexedDB + localStorage were cleared before tests.
context_for_next_testing_agent: After fix: clear IndexedDB+localStorage, login cashier/1234, open shift 500000, scan 8991111000011 + 8990000000226, pay cash. Then press Alt+R — modal must open without crashing and the just-paid transaction must appear in the left list WITH items visible on the right. Then test Alt+C and verify Expected Cash math, then submit and ensure redirect to /login. Also recheck StatusBar, F12 toast, F2 logout — they are likely fine, they only failed in iteration_1 because the React tree was crashed by ReturnModal.
rca of the issue: ReturnModal.jsx line 85 reads `t.items.length` for every transaction returned by api.listTransactions(). api.listTransactions reads db.transactions only (mockApi.js line 55). posStore.finalize (posStore.js lines 162-178) builds `trx` without an `items` field and persists the line items to db.transaction_items separately. Therefore every transaction header returned has items === undefined → reading .length throws → React unmounts the app → white screen. Reproduction: login → open shift → scan a product → pay → Alt+R. Mitigation: hydrate items in mockApi.listTransactions (await Promise.all(rows.map(async t => ({...t, items: await db.transaction_items.where('transactionId').equals(t.id).toArray()})))) OR persist items inline in db.transactions. Either change is sufficient.
